entries.title: Symptom Onset
entries.description: Patient, a 55-year-old postmenopausal female (G3P3), began experiencing intermittent postmenopausal bleeding.

sentences: Patient presented to her PCP with intermittent postmenopausal bleeding starting June 2025.

entries.title: Initial Imaging
entries.description: Transvaginal ultrasound showed thickened endometrial stripe at 14mm.

sentences: She underwent transvaginal ultrasound on 2025-07-20 showing thickened endometrial stripe at 14mm.

entries.title: Diagnostic Biopsy
entries.description: Office endometrial biopsy revealed Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma.

sentences: Office endometrial biopsy performed 2025-08-01 revealed Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma of the endometrium.
Reviewed pathology from office endometrial biopsy (2025-08-01): Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma

entries.title: Initial Oncology Consultation
entries.description: Patient referred to GYN oncology for surgical management. Strong family history of colon and endometrial cancer, raising suspicion for Lynch syndrome. Workup ordered: MMR IHC, CT imaging (chest/abdomen/pelvis), genetic referral, labs (CBC, CMP, CA-125, HE4). Planned for robotic-assisted hysterectomy, BSO, sentinel lymph node mapping.

sentences: Family history concerning for Lynch syndrome.
Recommend preoperative workup including MMR IHC, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis for staging, and genetic counseling referral.
Plan: Schedule for robotic-assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, sentinel lymph node mapping, and possible pelvic/para-aortic lymphadenectomy

entries.title: Molecular Tumor Testing – Office Biopsy
entries.description: MMR IHC on endometrial biopsy revealed loss of MLH1 and PMS2, intact MSH2/MSH6. ER: 40% positive, PR: 10% positive. MLH1/PMS2 loss pattern—potential Lynch or MLH1 methylation (testing pending). POLE mutation test pending. p53 wild-type.

sentences: IHC performed at outside facility: ER 40% positive, PR 10% positive
MMR IHC ordered and resulted today: MLH1: LOSS of expression, PMS2: LOSS of expression, MSH2: Intact expression, MSH6: Intact expression
POLE mutation testing pending
p53 IHC: wild-type pattern (normal staining)

entries.title: Genetic Counseling and Testing Initiated
entries.description: Genetic counseling: Amsterdam II and Bethesda criteria met. MLH1 promoter methylation testing: Negative. Comprehensive germline panel sent.

sentences: Patient seen for genetic counseling regarding concern for Lynch syndrome.
Amsterdam II criteria: MET (3 relatives with Lynch-associated cancers, 2 successive generations, 1 diagnosed <50)
Bethesda guidelines: MET (endometrial cancer <60 with MMR deficiency)
Tumor Testing Results: MLH1/PMS2 loss by IHC; MLH1 promoter methylation: NEGATIVE (not hypermethylated)
Germline Testing: Comprehensive Lynch syndrome panel sent (Invitae)

entries.title: Lynch Syndrome Confirmed
entries.description: Pathogenic MLH1 variant (c.350C>T, p.Thr117Met) identified on germline testing. Diagnosis: Lynch syndrome. Implications discussed including risk-reducing BSO, colonoscopy, and family cascade testing.

sentences: Results (2025-10-15): PATHOGENIC VARIANT detected in MLH1 gene - MLH1 c.350C>T (p.Thr117Met) - Known pathogenic variant
Diagnosis: Lynch Syndrome (MLH1 pathogenic variant)
Implications for cancer management: Endometrial cancer is Lynch-associated

entries.title: Definitive Surgery
entries.description: Robotic-assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, sentinel lymph node mapping and biopsy, peritoneal washings performed. Uterus 10-week size, no gross metastatic disease, successful mapping and removal of sentinel lymph nodes. No surgical complications.

sentences: Robotic-assisted total laparoscopic hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, bilateral sentinel lymph node mapping and biopsy, pelvic washings.
Findings: Uterus enlarged to approximately 10-week size; Bilateral ovaries and fallopian tubes grossly normal; No evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis or omental disease.
Sentinel lymph nodes identified (2 left pelvic, 1 right pelvic) – sent for ultrastaging.
Complications: None.

entries.title: Final Surgical Pathology
entries.description: Pathology confirms Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma. Deep myometrial invasion (60%), extensive LVSI, no cervical stromal invasion. Pelvic and para-aortic sentinel lymph nodes (3) negative (including immunostaining), peritoneal washings negative, ovaries/tubes negative. FIGO 2023: Stage IB; High-intermediate risk; Molecular: MMRd (Lynch syndrome, germline MLH1). Board to discuss adjuvant therapy.

sentences: - Grade 3 endometrioid adenocarcinoma
- Myometrial invasion: 60% (>50% - deep invasion)
- LVSI: Present (extensive)
- Cervical stromal invasion: Absent
- All 3 sentinel lymph nodes: NEGATIVE
- Peritoneal washings: Negative
- Both ovaries and tubes: No tumor
FIGO 2023 Stage: IB (deep myometrial invasion, no cervical/nodal/distant disease)
With molecular classification: Stage IBm-MMRd
High-intermediate risk: Grade 3, Deep invasion, LVSI, MMRd

entries.title: Multidisciplinary Tumor Board Discussion
entries.description: Tumor board consensus: Adjuvant vaginal cuff brachytherapy (3 fractions), consider carboplatin/paclitaxel x4 cycles given high-intermediate risk features (Grade 3, deep invasion, LVSI), discuss clinical trial options for adjuvant immunotherapy. Continue Lynch syndrome-directed cancer surveillance; follow-up every 3 months for 2 years.

sentences: Case presented: 55yo with FIGO Stage IBm-MMRd endometrioid adenocarcinoma.
Treatment Discussion: Standard care for high-intermediate risk: Vaginal cuff brachytherapy, consider chemotherapy, immunotherapy in trial setting.

Board Consensus: Adjuvant vaginal cuff brachytherapy (3 fractions); Consider adjuvant chemotherapy (carboplatin/paclitaxel x 4 cycles) given high-intermediate risk features; Discuss clinical trial eligibility for adjuvant immunotherapy trials; Continue Lynch syndrome surveillance.
Follow-up plan: Every 3 months x 2 years with exam and CA-125; Colonoscopy per Lynch protocol.
